tactical.rationale: RSI 56.5 is neutral-constructive; price is +2.29% and above both the 50d ($12.25) and 200d ($11.61) in a golden-cross configuration. Momentum is intact but not extended — no tactical edge to add over the quant's 0. Freight spot rates (Drewry WCI) have been drifting lower through mid-2026 as Red Sea capacity gradually normalizes and new vessel deliveries (record orderbook ~2024-2025 newbuilds) hit the water, which caps near-term upside on the tactical horizon. I lean marginally toward the trim side of neutral.

positional.rationale: The quant's +8 rests on GSCPI 1.25 being read as a 'freight revenue tailwind.' I partially disagree on interpretation: GSCPI at 1.25 reflects elevated supply-chain pressure that is largely a function of Red Sea rerouting (longer voyages absorbing capacity) rather than genuine demand strength. That tailwind is fragile — as rerouting normalizes and capacity returns, the GSCPI support and the freight rate premium both fade together. Price above both MAs is a real positional positive, but I discount the GSCPI signal, pulling me below the quant to roughly flat.
det_notes: GSCPI 1.25: elevated pressure — freight revenue tailwind

strategic.rationale: This is where the real value sits. P/B 0.70 for an asset-heavy fleet operator is below replacement cost — a structural buy signal per the cyclical rules, and it means the market is pricing the fleet at a discount to steel value. Trailing P/E 22x is genuinely mid-cycle (not trough, not peak) per the inverted framework, so no penalty. Crucially, the market habitually prices Maersk's Logistics & Services segment near zero during freight troughs, versus DSV (~20x EV/EBITDA) and Kuehne+Nagel (~15-20x) — a sum-of-parts gap that supports downside protection. Balance sheet is strong (net cash post-2021-22 supercycle), buybacks and a 2.8% yield provide a floor. HY OAS at ~269bps is tight, signalling a healthy trade/credit environment. I go modestly more bullish than the quant's -11.
det_notes: AMKBY P/B 0.70: below book — value territory
HY OAS 2.69bps: tight — healthy trade environment
GSCPI 1.25: above average — healthy freight demand

composite.summary: Quant sees mild positional tailwind from GSCPI which I discount as Red Sea rerouting artifact, but the strategic value case (P/B 0.70 below replacement, Logistics segment priced near zero vs DSV/K+N, strong balance sheet, 2.8% yield) is genuine and slightly better than the quant read. Net a modest HOLD leaning to accumulate on the strategic underpin.

positional.falsifier: GSCPI dropping below 0 (calm markets) combined with a break of the 50d SMA would flip this positive/trim; a sustained GSCPI move above 1.5 with rising spot rates would push it clearly negative/buy.

strategic.falsifier: A decisive structural down-cycle signal — ocean EBIT turning negative for consecutive quarters with management guiding to sustained losses, or P/B pushing back above ~1.0 on a rate spike removing the value cushion — would flip the sign.

positional.rationale: Engine maxes at -100 on below_both_death MA + 1% 52w position, but I materially discount this. Positional POOR reliability (30.4%) and yesterday's -77 positional BUY was wrong (price fell). The mechanical logic — 'deepest drawdown = max conviction' — is exactly the trap that gets punished when a stock is in a genuine multiple de-rate ahead of a feared 2027 transition. Being at the 52w low in a death-cross is NOT automatically bullish; it can mean the market is correctly repricing the I&A annuity risk. That said, trailing P/E 46.7x is genuinely low for this franchise (forward likely ~38-42x, well under the 45x fair zone), recurring revenue ~86%, and no confirmed procedure-share loss exists. So the fundamental floor is real. I land at -55: strong buy-lean but nowhere near the engine's -100, respecting that positional signals have been noise and the print could validate the de-rate.
